what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, replying to a prior point and agreeing that the dragnet's registries form a graph of typed edges at registry, type, and field levels; it also notes that related experiments are scattered in transcripts and expects the dragnet to help locate them.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, title='Tim · point', author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to board://item-bd30758f
Created and updated timestamps are identical
Body is a markdown comment tagged [point] and begins with 're: «The registries-are-also-a-graph...»'
Affirms that registries, types, and fields all have typed edges to other registries/types/fields
Describes this as an invariant or fractal pattern rather than a single prespecified idea
Mentions 'loose ideas and a lot of different experiments' discussed in transcripts, with no known location
States belief that the dragnet will be instrumental in finding those transcripts
standouts: Explicitly frames the dragnet index facts as subject–relationship–object graph statements
Describes typed-edge relationships as recurring/fractal across registry, type, and field levels
Links the value of the dragnet to recovering past experiment transcripts
concerns: Refers to transcripts and experiments that are not linked or identified in the file
Uses vague phrasing ('loose ideas', 'I don’t have a single prespecified idea') without concrete details
No code symbols or structured references to ground the claims
notes: Tim's note agreeing the dragnet index is inherently graph-shaped and hoping the tool will surface related experiment transcripts.
questions: Which transcripts contain the experiments Tim discussed?
What are the specific 'loose ideas' about registry/type/field graphs?
How exactly do registry, type, and field edges map to subject–relationship–object statements?
purpose_vs_actual: The frontmatter marks it as a note/comment on another item, and the body is a reflective agreement plus a forward-looking remark about transcript retrieval — it matches the stated purpose of a point/reply.
intention_signals: To record Tim's agreement that the dragnet's stored facts already constitute a graph and to signal a future use case: using the dragnet to find prior experiment discussions in transcripts.
novelty: Treats registry/type/field relationships as a self-similar pattern of typed edges and explicitly identifies the index's three-part facts as raw graph material.
contribution: Preserves a participant's assertion that the system's registries naturally form a graph and records an anticipated use case (locating experiment transcripts) for the dragnet.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting that registry, type, and field relationships form a fractal graph of typed edges and anticipating the dragnet will help locate related experiment transcripts.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement that the dragnet index is inherently graph-structured and to flag the need to surface past experiment discussions from transcripts.
